Build Your Language Bank

Page 1 of 8

Ask yourself: “Will I realistically use this in my own writing?”

Worth recording

Language that is reusable, natural, and easy to imagine in your own essays or speaking.

useful chunks collocations academic phrases

Usually skip

Names, dates, highly specific terms, or words you understand but probably won't use often.

too specific not reusable low priority
Example: You might understand a word like equity, but if you don't see yourself using it often, you can skip it for now. Your Language Bank should stay practical.

Let's Build One Together

Imagine you've just read this short article. Click each highlighted phrase to see why it is worth recording.

What is universal healthcare?
Universal healthcare seeks to make accessing healthcare a fundamental right for citizens of a country. Under universal healthcare, everyone can expect to receive the healthcare they need without financial difficulty.

Universal healthcare is said to date back to 19th century Germany, where a system was set up that required industrial employers to provide healthcare to workers. To do so, employees and employers contributed to “sickness funds,” which they used to pay for care.

Today, universal healthcare systems exist worldwide, including Australia, Canada, Japan and the United Kingdom.

Click a highlighted phrase

We'll decide whether it deserves a place in the Language Bank.

Our Language Bank

Useful phrases will appear here.

Why skip some words?
We don't need to record “sickness funds,” country names, or historical details. They help us understand this article, but most learners are unlikely to reuse them often.

How to Record Vocabulary

Keep it simple. Record the phrase, add a short meaning only if helpful, and save one good example.

Language Bank

Phrase

fundamental right

Meaning

basic human right

Example

Access to clean water is a fundamental right.
You don't need patterns, long notes, or five examples. The goal is to build something you will actually keep using.

Your Turn

Read the article independently first. Decide what you would add to your own Language Bank.

Pros of universal healthcare
Maybe the greatest benefit of universal healthcare is that it gives each citizen the ability to access healthcare services regardless of their ability to pay. The World Health Organization argues that healthcare is a fundamental right and that providing citizens with access to healthcare is one of the most important roles of a government.

Lower costs for both citizens and healthcare providers — Under a universal healthcare system, there’s no competition among health insurance companies. Instead, a single governing entity regulates healthcare costs. This can drive the cost of healthcare down substantially. Similarly, it reduces administrative costs for doctors and healthcare practitioners.

A universal healthcare system could save over $450 billion per year, according to a 2022 Yale study. This could help many, since polls have found that nearly 4 out of 10 Americans have delayed or gone without healthcare because of costs.
Before you compare, ask yourself: Which phrases would I realistically use in my own writing?

Compare with Mine

Now click each highlighted phrase to see why it may be worth recording.

Pros of universal healthcare
Maybe the greatest benefit of universal healthcare is that it gives each citizen the ability to access healthcare services regardless of their ability to pay. The World Health Organization argues that healthcare is a fundamental right and that providing citizens with access to healthcare is one of the most important roles of a government.

Lower costs for both citizens and healthcare providers — Under a universal healthcare system, there’s no competition among health insurance companies. Instead, a single governing entity regulates healthcare costs. This can drive the cost of healthcare down substantially. Similarly, it reduces administrative costs for doctors and healthcare practitioners.

A universal healthcare system could save over $450 billion per year, according to a 2022 Yale study. This could help many, since polls have found that nearly 4 out of 10 Americans have delayed or gone without healthcare because of costs.

Suggested Answers

  • access healthcare services — English naturally uses access + noun.
  • regardless of — A useful academic connector.
  • ability to pay — Learn the pattern ability to + verb.
  • fundamental right — Reusable across many topics.
  • one of the most important roles of... — A natural English way to emphasise importance.
  • delay or go without — A common native-speaker expression.

Click a phrase

You'll see why it is worth recording and how you can reuse it.

Where Should I Store My Notes?

You can use a paper notebook or a digital app. The important thing is to keep everything about the same topic together.

💡 One topic = one spread.
Put reusable language on the left, and examples, ideas or statistics on the right.
Remember:
Whether you use a notebook or an app, organise your notes by topic. Keep useful language together with useful examples, ideas and statistics so everything you need for that topic is in one place.

One Digital Option

If you use a digital notebook like Obsidian, you can organise notes with a parent → child structure.

Healthcare is the big topic. Under it, you can create smaller topics such as 1 Universal Healthcare and 2 Private vs Public Healthcare.
⌄ Healthcare
1 Universal Healthcare
1a access healthcare services
1b regardless of
1c fundamental right
1d Yale study example
2 Private vs Public Healthcare
2a waiting times
2b quality of care
› Education
› Environment
› Technology
Healthcare / 1 Universal Healthcare
1 Universal Healthcare
This is a smaller topic inside the bigger topic: Healthcare.
Everything you collect from one article about universal healthcare can live under this subtopic. Later, when you read a different article, you can create another subtopic such as 2 Private vs Public Healthcare.
Structure:
Healthcare = big topic
1 Universal Healthcare = smaller topic
1a, 1b, 1c, 1d = language, examples, or evidence from that topic
